title: 30-Day Social Media Calendar
description: Plan and create a complete 30-day social media content calendar for a brand, including post types, captions, visual direction, and posting schedule.
whyItMatters: Social media is often the first marketing channel a brand uses. Planning content in advance ensures consistency, saves time, and allows strategic thinking rather than reactive posting.
prerequisites: Familiarity with at least one social platform

steps.title: Define the brand and goals
steps.description: Choose a brand (real or fictional). Define their social media goals: awareness, engagement, traffic, or sales. Pick 1-2 platforms to focus on.

steps.title: Identify content pillars
steps.description: Choose 3-5 content themes that align with the brand and audience. Example: educational tips, behind-the-scenes, customer stories, product highlights, industry trends.
steps.tips: Content pillars prevent your feed from being all sales or all fluff. Aim for a mix.

steps.title: Plan the month
steps.description: Map out 30 days of posts. Decide posting frequency (e.g., 5x/week). Assign each post a pillar, format (image, carousel, video, story), and platform.

steps.title: Write captions
steps.description: Write the actual caption for every post. Include hooks (first line grabs attention), body text, and CTAs where appropriate. Add relevant hashtag suggestions.

steps.title: Create visual direction
steps.description: For each post, describe the visual (or create it in Canva). Maintain consistent brand colours and style.
steps.tips: You don't need to be a designer — Canva templates work great for this.

steps.title: Add engagement strategy
steps.description: Plan how you'll engage with comments, respond to DMs, and interact with other accounts. Schedule 15 minutes per day for community management.

deliverables: Visual mockups for at least 10 posts

rubric.description: Posts clearly support the stated goals and each pillar is represented.
Captions are engaging, on-brand, and include clear hooks and CTAs.
Visuals follow a cohesive brand style and feel professional.
The calendar is realistic and sustainable — not too sparse or too aggressive.
Content is appropriate for the chosen platform (not repurposed blindly).
